title: Kapkan notification payload (schema_version 1)
description: The JSON object POSTed to notify.webhook.url and piped to notify.exec.command's stdin on every attack lifecycle event. The exec hook also receives the event name as argv[1]. Fields marked optional are omitted when empty. Consumers MUST check schema_version and SHOULD ignore unknown fields for forward compatibility.

schema_version.description: Payload shape version. Bumped on breaking changes.

scope.description: host: a single address is under attack (target set). group: a calculation:total hostgroup's summed traffic (group set, target absent).

target.description: Attacked (or, for outgoing, attacking) host address. Absent for group-scoped events.

group.description: Owning hostgroup name; "global" for the implicit fallback group.

direction.description: outgoing means the target originates the attack (likely compromised).

metric.description: The threshold that triggered the detection.

rate.description: Observed value of the triggering metric (sampling-corrected).

threshold.description: Configured limit of the triggering metric at attack start.

ban_state.description: RTBH ban state, when mitigation was consulted.

classification.description: Attack vector inferred at detection time (attack_started only).

confidence.description: Share (0..1) of the attack traffic matching the winning signature; 0 for mixed (no signature matched).

src_port.description: Reflected service port, set for amplification vectors.

sample.description: Flow sample captured in the window before the threshold tripped (attack_started only, when the traffic buffer is enabled). Counter packets/bytes are sampling-corrected totals; raw flow records carry the exporter's pre-correction numbers plus sampling_rate.

total_packets.description: Untruncated sampling-corrected packet total of all matched flows; denominator for shares (the top-K lists drop lighter keys).
